Title: Bounded buffer-pool pressure
Purpose: Create a bounded, deterministic, synthetic bounded buffer-pool pressure state and verify the assigned framework analyzer without asserting host-dependent durations or exact wait counts.

Arrange.Action: Create only the exact synthetic Lab001Wave3 database and scenario-owned server objects from fixed seed 1701.
Arrange.CompletionSignal: The synthetic objects exist and the clean-state assertion succeeds.

Act.Action: Execute the bounded LAB-MEM-002 workload with at most 2 concurrent workers.
Act.CompletionSignal: The scenario-specific state predicate is observed within the declared timeout.

Observe.Scope: The exact synthetic database, run-token sessions, and explicitly named scenario-owned server objects.

AlternativeEvidence: A declared alternative state predicate is accepted when scheduler timing changes the transient wait or plan shape.
ExpectedFindings.AssertionBoundary: Exact waits, counters, durations, row ordering, and plan identifiers are never asserted.

AlternativeEvidence: Transient waits may differ; the scenario asserts the durable synthetic state and bounded live-session context instead.

Cleanup.Action: Kill only sessions carrying the exact run token, remove exact Lab001Wave3 server objects, and drop only the synthetic database.
Cleanup.CompletionSignal: No run-token session, scenario-owned server object, or Lab001Wave3 database remains.

KnownLimitations: The scenario is diagnostic evidence, not a benchmark or production sizing result.
Repository CI validates the action contract; real native-Linux and Hyper-V-Linux evidence remains an external gate.
